title: Iconic Park Entrance Sign for Vicente Perez Rosales National Park in Puerto Varas Chile
description: Puerto Varas, Los Lagos: A circular, weathered sign mounted on horizontal wood-paneled siding marks the entrance to Parque Nacional Vicente Perez Rosales. The insignia displays a stylized, colorful illustration of the region's dramatic landscape, featuring snow-capped volcanic peaks rising above deep green forests and cascading blue waterfalls. Sharp focus highlights the printed text and the vibrant, primary-color aesthetic of the logo against the muted, earthy tones of the rustic timber wall. Soft, diffused lighting provides an even illumination across the metal plaque, allowing the intricate graphic details to stand out clearly against the wood grain textures. This emblem serves as a gateway marker for travelers embarking on the iconic Cruce Andino journey, representing the pristine natural beauty of the Chilean Lake District. The juxtaposition of the man-made sign against the naturalistic wooden background emphasizes the intersection of conservation and tourism in one of South America’s most protected ecological zones. Such markers are vital touchstones for navigation and historical recognition in the region, connecting visitors to the vast wilderness of the Andes. This visual documentation captures the quiet anticipation of wilderness exploration found throughout this mountainous landscape. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
